## SCATTERPLOT MATRIX chart, KATA style

Render as SVG (or equivalent) on a 960x540 px canvas.
Every position, size, and color below is exact. No adjectives, no improvisation.

### AI metadata (for agent routing)
```json
{
  "code": "CH-COR-07",
  "ovp_version": "1.0",
  "intent": [
    "correlation",
    "pattern"
  ],
  "form": "3x3 grid of mini scatters, every variable against every other",
  "data_required": "exactly 3 measures over up to 30 items; overview before analysis",
  "family": "COR",
  "alternatives": [
    "CH-COR-01 (the one pair that matters)",
    "CH-COR-05 (many points, one pair)"
  ],
  "see_instead": [
    {
      "when": "one pair is already known to matter",
      "use": "CH-COR-01"
    },
    {
      "when": "hundreds of points",
      "use": "CH-COR-05"
    }
  ]
}
```

Use when:
- WARN: analyst overview to pick which pairwise plot deserves a real chart
- screening 3 measures for relationships in one glance

Do NOT use when:
- presenting to any non-analyst (36 tiny axes explain nothing)
- more than 3 variables in this spec (use small multiples of CH-COR-01)

### Rules
- diagonal cells name the variable and its 0-to-top range
- no ticks in cells: this is a screening view, not a reading view
- the highlight item is visible in every panel

### Never do this
- WARN ENTRY: a SPLOM is a workbench, not a deliverable; ship CH-COR-01 of the pair that matters
- gridlines and ticks in mini cells
- 4+ variables

### QA before delivering
- [ ] exactly 3 variables
- [ ] <= 30 points
- [ ] diagonal states ranges
- [ ] source present

### Colors (resolved, use these exact values)

| Element | Color |
|---|---|
| background | `#FFFFFF` |
| frame | `#E2E8E8` |
| dot | `#B7DCDC` |
| dot_highlight | `#008080` |
| axis_label | `#555555` |
| tick_label | `#555555` |
| title | `#222222` |
| source | `#555555` |

### Fonts

- Title font: 'Rethink Sans','Segoe UI',sans-serif, weight 600
- All other text: 'Poppins','Segoe UI',sans-serif, weight 400 (600 on highlighted items)

### Language chrome

No kicker, no eyebrow; title at the default y.
